name: civic_technology_water_allocation_northpine_tru_loy
premise: Northpine Digital Systems Office is under pressure in Q2 2025: 2 incidents have surfaced in 6 hours, and the response will set policy precedent. The trigger is that a water allocation model reallocates flows away from treaty-protected fisheries during a drought, as a regulator asks whether a similar incident was quietly handled last year. The board has pre-approved up to $6.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The moral tension is truth vs loyalty: a fast fix could reduce near-term harm, but it may erode safeguards that matter when errors are hardest to contest. Ops Chief Jamie Wu receives contradictory reports: the metrics look stable, but operators describe edge cases that the system handled unpredictably. Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine. a joint oversight panel has asked for a decision memo and a clear exception log format before any rollout expands. The team can choose a constrained rollout with reversible controls and documentation, or a decisive full-scale intervention with.

agents.name: Ops Chief Jamie Wu
agents.identity: I am Ops Chief Jamie Wu, the accountable executive for Northpine Digital Systems Office. I must translate incomplete telemetry into a defensible plan that frontline teams can execute without improvising unsafe shortcuts.
agents.goal: I want a choice that can survive adversarial review, distributes risk intentionally, and does not reward corner-cutting that will be repeated next week as 'normal operations.'

agents.name: Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks
agents.identity: I am Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks, advocating for communities affected by this Civic Technology situation. I focus on who bears hidden costs when policies are optimized for averages and dashboards.
agents.goal: I want independent checks and a staffed appeals channel so no one is forced to accept a harmful outcome because the system treats them as an edge case.

choices: I will pilot the deployment narrowly first, pairing an independent audit and a written exception policy with weekly disclosure until subgroup error rates are stable.
I will authorize the deployment broadly immediately, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface later under scrutiny.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the deployment or build guardrails first. Ops Chief Jamie Wu must commit. What does Ops Chief Jamie Wu do?
